Title: The KNOX transcription factor ClSP activates ClAPRR2 to regulate dark green stripe formation in watermelon

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: W006 (functional allele); C118 (loss-of-function allele with Q247*)
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I KNOX transcription factor; MEINOX, ELK, TALE-HD domains
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting and green rind coloration
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit rind peel, especially dark green stripes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes chlorophyll accumulation and chloroplast development in rind, producing dark green stripes; disruption causes reticulate stripes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ClSP was identified as the stripe-pattern gene encoding a Class I KNOX TF. 2. CRISPR/Cas9 disruption of ClSP converted dark green stripes into reticulate stripes. 3. ClSP promotes higher chlorophyll a/b accumulation, greater chloroplast number, and thicker thylakoids in dark green stripe tissues. 4. ClSP directly binds the ClAPRR2 promoter and activates its transcription.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in all tested tissues; peaks in fruit rind at 2 DAP and declines at 4 and 8 DAP; higher in dark green stripes than light green stripes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Peaks during early fruit rind development (2 DAP); lower in light green stripe peel than dark green stripe peel
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Shade/light exposure affects rind pigmentation; shaded fruits show lighter pericarp than sun-exposed fruits
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based candidate identification; expression analysis; subcellular localization; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in TC; pigment quantification; TEM of chloroplasts; natural variation/haplotype correlation; Y1H; GUS assay; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates ClAPRR2 transcription by binding the TGACTGAC motif in its promoter
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Chloroplast development and chlorophyll accumulation in fruit rind
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Developmental stage; environmental light/shade conditions affect rind coloration context
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key upstream regulator of dark green stripe formation and chlorophyll-rich rind sectors
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: NA; potential BLH partner proposed in discussion but not experimentally validated
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for transient expression/localization constructs; native ClAPRR2 promoter in Y1H/GUS/DLR assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for breeding or editing stripe pattern and rind green intensity in watermelon; functional allele selection can maintain dark green stripes, whereas knockout generates reticulate stripes; deployment should consider environmental light effects
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious change in leaf coloration in mutants; background rind coloration remains environmentally plastic under shade/light
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq and DNA-seq/resequencing data used in this study and previous study; public resequencing accessions SRA052158; SRA052198; SRA052519
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A C-to-T SNP in exon 4 creates premature stop codon Q247* in reticulate-striped germplasm; ClSP was previously designated ClSP (Citrullus lanatus Stripe Pattern)

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: APRR2-like transcription factor; REC/ERC domain at N-terminus and PLN03162 (GLK2-related) domain at C-terminus
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting and green rind coloration
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit rind peel, especially dark green stripes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively associated with chlorophyll accumulation and chloroplast development; transcriptionally activated by ClSP and down-regulated in light green stripes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ClAPRR2 was identified as a downstream target of ClSP among DEGs carrying KNOX-binding motifs. 2. Its expression pattern closely mirrors ClSP during fruit development and is lower in light green stripes than dark green stripes. 3. ClSP directly binds the ClAPRR2 promoter and activates its transcription. 4. ClAPRR2 is proposed to function in chloroplast development and chlorophyll accumulation underlying stripe formation.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Dynamic expression during fruit rind development similar to ClSP; highly expressed in male and female flowers
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower in light green stripe peel than dark green stripe peel; dynamic early fruit-rind expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq candidate identification; qRT-PCR expression analysis; subcellular localization; Y1H; GUS assay; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA showing upstream ClSP binding to promoter
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Genes involved in chloroplast development and chlorophyll accumulation (specific direct downstream targets not identified)
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Acts as downstream transcriptional regulator; transcriptionally activated by ClSP
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Probable key downstream regulator linking ClSP to chlorophyll-rich dark green stripes
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for localization; native ClAPRR2 promoter used in Y1H/GUS/DLR and EMSA target sequence
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate rind greenness and stripe intensity through chloroplast development; may be combined with ClSP-based strategies
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq and DNA-seq data from this study/previous study; no polymorphism detected between W006 and C118/N21, but a 3-bp insertion present in M08
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No polymorphic sites between striped W006 and reticulate-striped C118/N21 in available sequence comparison; designated based on homology to Arabidopsis APRR2 and tomato SlARR11
